# Transcrição: Conversation_Hack__Ask_3_Follow-up_Questions

## [00:00 - 01:51]

Do you ever get stuck on what to say when talking to others? When I'm in a conversation with someone and I sense a peak emotion, that's usually my cue to ask three follow-up questions. This strategy not only enriches your relationships, it also enhances your own emotional intelligence. Let's consider your spending time with a friend and you ask them, hey, how's your day going? And your friend replies with, it's been okay. I've been feeling a little more stressed than usual. Many people would just respond with, ah, that sucks. I hope things get better soon. And if you reply like this, you just missed an opportunity to truly connect with the person. Instead, learn to be more attentive to the peak emotions that exist within your conversations. If I were to bring back the example conversation in slow motion, see if you can pick up the peak emotion. It's been okay. I've been feeling a little more stressed than usual. Clearly in that example, it was stress. In this case, then I would ask three questions to get to the heart of why they're feeling stressed. For example, I could ask, what's causing you the stress? What else is making you feel stressed? Is there more to the story? I love this one because it's an open-ended question and they can take the conversation anywhere they want. This is more than just the conversational trick. It's a wonderful way to deepen your relationships with the people you care about. And the more you practice this, the more you'll be in tune with people's emotions, which makes you more emotionally intelligent, which is pretty rare these days. If you want more conversational hacks, let me know in the comments below. And here's a side mission for you. Leave an emoji that you feel represents me best. Oh no, what have I done? I think I'm going to be really heartbroken by some of the emojis that I see below. But you know what? Let's lean into this discomfort. Craig, you leave me an emoji on this edit with what you think represents me best.
